title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for semantic html rendering, contextual escaping, and determinism and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
Encode untrusted text and attribute values for their exact HTML context and reject unsafe script, style, comment, and URL placements.
Keep trusted renderer output distinct from untrusted author text and make every conversion into trusted markup explicit and reviewable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Render Determinism
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz publishing team handles case 2466 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Render Determinism.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds. Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Renderer Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Renderer Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Renderer Contract, keep Renderer Contract connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz publishing team handles case 5372 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Render Determinism
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Render Determinism: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 6991 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Render Determinism.
steps.why: Retrieving Render Determinism through the concrete deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Render Determinism, keep Render Determinism connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.
